verses.text: They sail for Italy; Julius the centurion treats Paul kindly. A kind keeper. God gives His servant favour even in custody.
They sail for Italy; Julius the centurion treats Paul kindly. A kind keeper. God gives His servant favour even in custody.
They sail for Italy; Julius the centurion treats Paul kindly. A kind keeper. God gives His servant favour even in custody.
Sailing against the wind, they reach the fair havens with difficulty. Hard going. The voyage is troubled from the start.
Sailing against the wind, they reach the fair havens with difficulty. Hard going. The voyage is troubled from the start.
Sailing against the wind, they reach the fair havens with difficulty. Hard going. The voyage is troubled from the start.
Sailing against the wind, they reach the fair havens with difficulty. Hard going. The voyage is troubled from the start.
Sailing against the wind, they reach the fair havens with difficulty. Hard going. The voyage is troubled from the start.
Paul warns of loss; but the centurion believes the master, and they sail on. Counsel ignored. They scorn the warning to their cost.
Paul warns of loss; but the centurion believes the master, and they sail on. Counsel ignored. They scorn the warning to their cost.
Paul warns of loss; but the centurion believes the master, and they sail on. Counsel ignored. They scorn the warning to their cost.
Paul warns of loss; but the centurion believes the master, and they sail on. Counsel ignored. They scorn the warning to their cost.
A tempestuous wind, Euroclydon, catches the ship and drives it. The storm strikes. The fair south wind turns to fury.
A tempestuous wind, Euroclydon, catches the ship and drives it. The storm strikes. The fair south wind turns to fury.
A tempestuous wind, Euroclydon, catches the ship and drives it. The storm strikes. The fair south wind turns to fury.
They lighten the ship; for many days no sun or stars; 'all hope... was then taken away.' Despair. Sailors and soldiers give themselves up for lost.
They lighten the ship; for many days no sun or stars; 'all hope... was then taken away.' Despair. Sailors and soldiers give themselves up for lost.
They lighten the ship; for many days no sun or stars; 'all hope... was then taken away.' Despair. Sailors and soldiers give themselves up for lost.
They lighten the ship; for many days no sun or stars; 'all hope... was then taken away.' Despair. Sailors and soldiers give themselves up for lost.
They lighten the ship; for many days no sun or stars; 'all hope... was then taken away.' Despair. Sailors and soldiers give themselves up for lost.
Ye should have hearkened; yet be of good cheer, no loss of life, only the ship. A word of hope. Paul comforts where all despair.
Ye should have hearkened; yet be of good cheer, no loss of life, only the ship. A word of hope. Paul comforts where all despair.
'There stood by me this night the angel of God... Fear not'; thou must stand before Caesar, and God hath given thee all. Sure promise. God's word steadies the ship's company.
'There stood by me this night the angel of God... Fear not'; thou must stand before Caesar, and God hath given thee all. Sure promise. God's word steadies the ship's company.
'There stood by me this night the angel of God... Fear not'; thou must stand before Caesar, and God hath given thee all. Sure promise. God's word steadies the ship's company.
'There stood by me this night the angel of God... Fear not'; thou must stand before Caesar, and God hath given thee all. Sure promise. God's word steadies the ship's company.
At midnight the shipmen sense land and cast anchors, wishing for day. Anxious watch. Danger presses as the shore nears.
At midnight the shipmen sense land and cast anchors, wishing for day. Anxious watch. Danger presses as the shore nears.
At midnight the shipmen sense land and cast anchors, wishing for day. Anxious watch. Danger presses as the shore nears.
The shipmen would flee; 'Except these abide... ye cannot be saved'; the soldiers cut the boat loose. Means and trust. Faith does not despise the necessary means.
The shipmen would flee; 'Except these abide... ye cannot be saved'; the soldiers cut the boat loose. Means and trust. Faith does not despise the necessary means.
The shipmen would flee; 'Except these abide... ye cannot be saved'; the soldiers cut the boat loose. Means and trust. Faith does not despise the necessary means.
Paul urges them to eat, gives thanks, and breaks bread before all. Calm leader. He cares for their bodies and honours God openly.
Paul urges them to eat, gives thanks, and breaks bread before all. Calm leader. He cares for their bodies and honours God openly.
Paul urges them to eat, gives thanks, and breaks bread before all. Calm leader. He cares for their bodies and honours God openly.
All take courage and eat; two hundred seventy-six souls; they lighten the ship. Renewed cheer. His confidence spreads to all aboard.
All take courage and eat; two hundred seventy-six souls; they lighten the ship. Renewed cheer. His confidence spreads to all aboard.
All take courage and eat; two hundred seventy-six souls; they lighten the ship. Renewed cheer. His confidence spreads to all aboard.
They run the ship aground; the forepart sticks, the hinder breaks with the waves. Wreck. The ship is lost as Paul foretold.
They run the ship aground; the forepart sticks, the hinder breaks with the waves. Wreck. The ship is lost as Paul foretold.
They run the ship aground; the forepart sticks, the hinder breaks with the waves. Wreck. The ship is lost as Paul foretold.
The soldiers would kill the prisoners, but the centurion spares them; all escape safe. Promise kept. Not one life is lost, as God said.
The soldiers would kill the prisoners, but the centurion spares them; all escape safe. Promise kept. Not one life is lost, as God said.
